chunks.title: Bildad's Initial Rebuke and Promise
chunks.reason: Bildad opens the speech by questioning Job's words and asserting God's justice before offering a conditional promise of restoration.

chunks.title: Appeal to Tradition and the Fate of the Wicked
chunks.reason: Bildad appeals to ancestral wisdom and uses a metaphor about papyrus to illustrate the inevitable end of those who forget God.

chunks.title: Description of the Wicked and Final Exhortation
chunks.reason: The speech concludes with a detailed description of the fragile hope of the godless followed by a final assurance of God's justice.
